title: Diagnose and fix load error preventing game initialization

requirements.text: Add detailed error tracking to capture and log the specific load error occurring on deployment, including stack traces, component mount failures, and any React Three Fiber or Physics initialization errors

quotes: THERE SEEMS TO BE A LOAD ERROR

acceptanceCriteria: Console logs clearly identify the source of the load error
Error messages include component names and lifecycle stage where failure occurs
Stack traces are preserved and logged for debugging

requirements.text: Verify that all required dependencies for React Three Fiber Canvas and Physics provider are properly loaded before attempting to render the 3D scene
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas only renders after all Three.js dependencies are confirmed available
Physics provider initialization is validated before use
Loading screen displays until all critical dependencies are ready

requirements.text: Add fallback rendering logic that displays a meaningful error message to the user if the 3D canvas fails to initialize, rather than showing a blank screen
acceptanceCriteria: User sees a clear error message explaining the load failure
Error message includes actionable steps (e.g., 'Please refresh the page')
Fallback UI renders even when 3D initialization fails

requirements.text: Implement retry logic that automatically attempts to reinitialize the game if the initial load fails, with a maximum of 3 retry attempts before showing a permanent error state
acceptanceCriteria: Game automatically retries initialization on failure
User is informed of retry attempts through loading screen message
After 3 failed attempts, a permanent error message displays with manual reload option

requirements.text: Ensure the ErrorBoundary component properly catches and displays errors from the Canvas, Physics provider, and all child 3D components with detailed error information
acceptanceCriteria: ErrorBoundary catches all React Three Fiber rendering errors
Error display includes component stack and error message
Reload button in ErrorBoundary successfully clears error state and reinitializes

constraints: Do not modify Internet Identity authentication hooks (useInternetIdentity.ts, useInternetIdentity.tsx, useActor.ts)
Do not modify main.tsx entry point
Do not modify UI components in frontend/src/components/ui directory
nonGoals: Changing the core game mechanics or controls
Modifying the visual aesthetic or art style
Changing the backend state management structure
